name: preprocessing_scaling_behavior
description: How scalers handle data types, sparse matrices, and fitting/transforming edge cases
seed_rules: StandardScaler centers data to mean=0 and scales to unit variance by default
Calling transform() before fit() raises a NotFittedError
StandardScaler with_mean=True cannot be applied to sparse matrices because centering destroys sparsity

name: cross_validation_data_leakage
description: How Pipeline, ColumnTransformer, and CV interact to prevent or inadvertently allow data leakage
seed_rules: Wrapping a scaler inside a Pipeline ensures the scaler is fit only on training folds during cross-validation
Fitting a scaler on the full dataset before cross_val_score causes data leakage even if the model is not pre-fit
StratifiedKFold preserves class proportions across folds for classification tasks

name: random_state_reproducibility
description: Which estimators and functions require random_state and when results are truly deterministic
seed_rules: Setting random_state=42 in an estimator makes repeated calls to fit() produce identical results
train_test_split with the same random_state always produces the same split regardless of input order
RandomForestClassifier with random_state set is reproducible, but only when n_jobs=1; parallel jobs can alter output ordering

name: feature_importance_interpretation
description: Ordering guarantees, bias, and meaning of feature_importances_ across different estimators
seed_rules: feature_importances_ in tree-based models sums to 1.0 across all features
Higher feature_importances_ means the feature is more useful for prediction in all cases
Permutation importance and Gini importance can produce different rankings especially for high-cardinality features

name: nan_handling_in_estimators
description: How NaN values are handled, propagated, or rejected across different estimators and transformers
seed_rules: Most scikit-learn estimators raise a ValueError if NaN values are present in the input data
SimpleImputer with strategy='mean' replaces NaNs with the column mean computed during fit
HistGradientBoostingClassifier natively supports NaN values without imputation, unlike GradientBoostingClassifier

name: predict_proba_calibration
description: When predicted probabilities from classifiers are well-calibrated versus miscalibrated by default
seed_rules: predict_proba() returns values between 0 and 1 that can be interpreted as probabilities
SVC does not support predict_proba by default; it must be enabled with probability=True which uses Platt scaling
RandomForestClassifier's predicted probabilities tend to be pushed away from 0 and 1 and are not automatically well-calibrated
